summary: Iter 78c labor-lock policy change — LP labor is now editable while Vinyl + Ascend stay locked. All 6 acceptance criteria PASS. Backend pytest 29/29 (test_hover_perimeter + test_pricing_parity + test_j_channel_formula + test_lp_waste_roundtrip). UI verified across LP (lab editable, persists), Vinyl (lab read-only $0), Windows (lab editable). DOM-level audit of 61 LP labor inputs confirms readOnly=false, tabIndex=0 (default), no 'cursor-not-allowed'. DOM-level audit of vinyl labs confirms readOnly=true, tabIndex=-1, cursor-not-allowed, bg-[#FAFAFA] text-[#A1A1AA] styling intact. End-to-end persistence proven by setting lab=9.99 on '38 Series Lap 3/8" x 8" x 16'' line (qty=255), waiting for 2s debounced autosave, then verifying via /api/estimates/{id} GET that lab=9.99 persisted; total recomputed to $11,056.80 = 255 × ($33.37 + $9.99). Orange reset-badge override marker rendered as expected on the overridden labor cell.
action_items: Optional cleanup: the LP estimate EST-723351 (id f27a3c6f-00c6-4401-94a0-33f22eb6212e) was left with qty=255 / lab=$9.99 on the 38 Series Lap 3/8" x 8" x 16' line as test residue. Howard can reset to lab=$0 via the orange ↺ badge when he opens it next.

Observation (not blocking): backend services.py:707-717 startup migration still includes 'lp_smart' in the lab=0 wipe filter. This is a one-shot startup migration so it won't actively clobber in-session edits — but it WILL nuke every contractor's saved LP labor on every backend restart. If Howard's plan is for LP labor edits to survive deploys/restarts, the 'lp_smart' entry must be removed from BOTH array_filters AND the $elemMatch tab list at services.py lines 709 + 714. (For ad-hoc testing this didn't surface because the backend wasn't restarted between save and GET, but next deploy will erase all per-line LP labor values.)
critical_code_review_comments: SectionAccordion.jsx:292 — branch now correctly reads ["vinyl","ascend"].includes(l.tab) ? readOnly : editable. Verified live: 61 LP labor inputs render via the editable branch (line 304-326). Vinyl + Ascend tabs hit the locked branch (line 293-301) with bg-[#FAFAFA] text-[#A1A1AA] cursor-not-allowed, tabIndex=-1, readOnly=true.

STARTUP MIGRATION INCONSISTENCY (services.py:707-717): The Iter-69 lab=0 backfill still targets tab IN ('vinyl','ascend','lp_smart'). The frontend lock was relaxed for lp_smart but the backend migration was not. Behavior today: in-session edits persist (verified end-to-end), but on next backend restart `ensure_tiers_seeded()` will wipe every lp_smart line's lab back to 0. RECOMMEND: drop 'lp_smart' from both $elemMatch and array_filters at lines 709/714 to match the new frontend policy.

useEstimate.js autosave is debounced at 2000ms (line 494). Tests that don't blur the input + wait ≥3s see the [pagehide-flush] keepalive flush fail on a reload — fine for end users (they don't usually reload mid-debounce) but worth a note for the testing playbook.

Backend PUT /estimates/{id} (routes/estimates.py:432) does NOT apply any per-request lab-zeroing — verified by direct curl PUT with lab=12 round-tripping cleanly. Backend-side acceptance of non-zero LP lab is confirmed.
seed_data_creation: None new. Used existing LP estimate EST-723351 (id f27a3c6f-00c6-4401-94a0-33f22eb6212e). Left '38 Series Lap 3/8" x 8" x 16'' with qty=255 / lab=$9.99 (was qty=0 / lab=$0 originally before testing).
context_for_next_testing_agent: LP labor editability + Vinyl/Ascend lock + Windows editable all verified. End-to-end persistence confirmed via /api/estimates GET round-trip. Autosave debounce is 2000ms — wait ≥5s after blur before reloading. Use page.evaluate with CSS.escape to handle data-testid values containing quotes (e.g. '38 Series Lap 3/8"...'). To set value in a way that triggers React state, use the native setter pattern: Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(HTMLInputElement.prototype,'value').set.call(el, v); el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})). One latent risk for next iteration: backend services.py:707-717 still wipes lp_smart lab on startup — flag for Howard if LP labor needs to survive deploys.

verified_acceptance_criteria.T3_lp_labor_type_and_persist: PASS — typed 9.99 on '38 Series Lap 3/8" x 8" x 16'' (qty=255), waited 6s, fetched /api/estimates/{id} → lab field present with 9.99; total recomputed to $11,056.80 = 255 × ($33.37+$9.99); orange override ↺ badge rendered
verified_acceptance_criteria.T4_vinyl_ascend_regression_locked: PASS — all 5 inspected Vinyl 'Conquest/Coventry' labor inputs: readOnly=true, tabIndex=-1, value=0, class contains 'cursor-not-allowed' + 'bg-[#FAFAFA]' + 'text-[#A1A1AA]'; programmatic value-set attempt was rejected (value stayed 0, readOnly stayed true)
verified_acceptance_criteria.T5_windows_regression_editable: PASS — 3 inspected Window Installation labor inputs ('Window DH/Slider - Pocket Install', 'Window - Full Fin Replacement', 'Large Window adder'): readOnly=false, tabIndex=0, no cursor-not-allowed
verified_acceptance_criteria.T6_backend_pytest_29_pass: PASS — 29 passed in 9.43s; junit xml at /app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iteration18.xml

rca_of_the_issue: Iter 78c is a one-line policy relaxation in SectionAccordion.jsx (removed 'lp_smart' from the tab whitelist that renders the read-only $0 lab cell). Frontend now routes LP labor through the same editable branch as Windows; Vinyl + Ascend remain locked. Backend ALREADY accepted any non-negative lab on lp_smart lines via PUT /estimates/{id} — no backend change was strictly required. CAVEAT: The Iter-69 startup migration in services.py:707-717 still includes 'lp_smart' in its tab filter. This will not affect contractors during a normal session, but the next backend restart will wipe every saved LP-line lab back to 0. Recommend dropping 'lp_smart' from both $elemMatch and array_filters there to fully align the backend with Howard's new policy.
